author: barnabasbusa
content: We can make a release as soon as the

author: barnabasbusa
content: ~~<@755590043632140352> did you guys rebase this on the devnet branch?~~ yes

author: nflaig
content: seems like CI was green but my PR lost the merge race and there were conflicts with some other PR you merged to `develop`, I resolved them, but worth re-reviewing that part

author: tersec
content: I still don't breoadly understand the point of having a bunch of non-"reserved" (to use Potuz's phrasing) builder versions which could be random garbage. what would they do, and wouldn't they hinder ever reusing those versions?

author: tersec
content: it's just immediately throwing away a lot of the possible builder-version-space

author: nflaig
content: https://github.com/ethereum/consensus-specs/pull/5435 what about reserving like this? we should just decide something

author: nflaig
content: status quo is not good to not reserve anything

author: tersec
content: yes, 100% agree

author: tersec
content: but my point is that to me, "allow"/"reserve" aren't separate lists. they're exact complements of each other. whatever isn't allowed (and given semantics) is prohibited

author: tersec
content: it's why I disagree with the "allow"/"reserve" framing

author: tersec
content:
> Allow builders to desposit with whatever version they want at the current fork as long as it's not reserved, potentially allowing some offchain mechanisms that would otherwise be forbidden.
> 
> Allow the protocol to create new builders for fork N+1 that can be deposited during fork N but that are required to never have landed a bid onchain before the fork.
for why non-"allowed"-non-"reserved" version should exist was never specified beyond <@755590043632140352> handwaving where he appears not to have anything particular in mind (?), and has a major cost in terms of this forever-removal-of-builder-versions

author: tersec
content: it's not some casual who-cares

author: tersec
content: and especially not something to throw away because we need a devnet tomorrow or whatever

author: barnabasbusa
content: ping on this. We wanna start testing teku as a buildoor

author: tersec
content: so the thing one can do right now, that does _not_ close off future options, and _does_ allow for decisions to be made in the future, is this exact-complement: allow the one builder version with semantics today, prohibit the rest

author: nflaig
content:
> allow the one builder version with semantics today
you do wanna allow a range though, not stricly just one version, otherwise we have the same problem again that you cannot onboard new builders before the next fork

author: tersec
content: The issue here was that a specific service (MEV) being used and which, for whatever reasons (not to retread that discussion) people think is "critical" continue through an otherwise finalization-long gap, already has a builder setup which ePBS moves into protocol

author: tersec
content: this is a special case

author: aarshshahoffchain
content:
Hey

Looks like the EL `GetBlobsV3` API/blobpool is broken in the latest (`ethpandaops/nethermind:glamsterdam-devnet-6`) nethermind image for devnet-6.
When I use the latest nethermind image, the CL repeatedly runs into a bunch of   `empty engine_getBlobsV3` responses which causes missed blocks. A bunch of the txpools contain zero blob transactions even though the spamoor is working well.

However, when I use the previous docker image of nethermind, everything works fine (with no changes at all in the CL code).
The previous docker image of nethermind that works well is: 

`ethpandaops/nethermind@sha256:04759d872c2ce1573be210806ece03e13445151c32955d21756d584364ee748e`

Here is my Kurtosis config file (this has the latest Nethermind image that's regressed).

author: aarshshahoffchain
content:
```
participants:
  - el_type: nethermind
    el_image: ethpandaops/nethermind:glamsterdam-devnet-6
    cl_type: prysm
    cl_image: prysm-bn-custom-image:latest
    vc_type: prysm
    vc_image: prysm-vc-custom-image:latest
    cl_log_level: "debug"
    count: 2
    validator_count: 128
    cl_extra_params:
      - --supernode
      - --subscribe-all-subnets
      - --partial-data-columns
    vc_extra_params:
      - --verbosity=debug
  - el_type: nethermind
    el_image: ethpandaops/nethermind:glamsterdam-devnet-6
    cl_type: prysm
    cl_image: prysm-bn-custom-image:latest
    vc_type: prysm
    vc_image: prysm-vc-custom-image:latest
    cl_log_level: "debug"
    count: 2
    validator_count: 4
    cl_extra_params:
      - --partial-data-columns
    vc_extra_params:
      - --verbosity=debug
  - el_type: nethermind
    el_image: ethpandaops/nethermind:glamsterdam-devnet-6
    cl_type: prysm
    cl_image: prysm-bn-custom-image:latest
    vc_image: prysm-vc-custom-image:latest
    count: 1
    validator_count: 0
    cl_extra_params:
      - --supernode
      - --partial-data-columns
      - --subscribe-all-subnets
    vc_extra_params:
      - --verbosity=debug

network_params:
  gloas_fork_epoch: 1
  preset: mainnet
  min_validator_withdrawability_delay: 1
  shard_committee_period: 1
  churn_limit_quotient: 16
  seconds_per_slot: 12
  gas_limit: 150000000

additional_services:
  - dora
  - spamoor

spamoor_params:
  image: ethpandaops/spamoor:master
  spammers:
    - scenario: eoatx
      config:
        throughput: 10
    - scenario: blobs
      config:
        throughput: 6

dora_params:
  image: ethpandaops/dora:glamsterdam-devnet-6

global_log_level: debug
```

author: aarshshahoffchain
content: For Prysm, you can use the latest at https://github.com/OffchainLabs/prysm/pull/17065 to build the Prysm Beacon and Validator node images to reproduce this.
